tasks.title: MCP Server Foundation Setup
tasks.description: Initialize MCP-compatible server architecture with basic tool registration and Claude Desktop integration
tasks.details: Create TypeScript/Node.js MCP server using @modelcontextprotocol/sdk. Implement server initialization, tool registration framework, and basic communication protocols. Set up package.json with MCP dependencies, create server.ts with MCP ServerOptions, implement tool discovery and registration system. Configure logging and error handling infrastructure. Test basic connection to Claude Desktop via MCP protocol.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for server initialization, integration tests for MCP protocol communication, manual testing of Claude Desktop connection and tool discovery

subtasks.title: TypeScript Project Initialization
subtasks.description: Set up TypeScript project structure with MCP SDK dependencies and build configuration
subtasks.details:
Initialize npm project with TypeScript support. Install @modelcontextprotocol/sdk and required dependencies (express, ws, etc.). Configure tsconfig.json with strict typing and module resolution. Set up package.json scripts for build, dev, and test commands. Create project structure with src/, dist/, tests/ directories. Configure ESLint and Prettier for code quality. Set up nodemon for development hot-reloading.
<info added on 2025-08-07T07:16:47.672Z>
**COMPLETION STATUS: FULLY COMPLETED**

Project initialization successfully finished with all required components:
- npm project initialized with TypeScript configuration
- MCP SDK (@modelcontextprotocol/sdk) and all dependencies installed
- tsconfig.json configured with strict typing and proper module resolution
- Complete package.json with build, dev, and test scripts
- Project structure created: src/, dist/, tests/ directories
- ESLint and Prettier configured and operational
- Jest testing framework set up
- Nodemon configured for development hot-reloading
- All tests passing - project ready for MCP server implementation

Ready to proceed to subtask 1.2 (MCP Server Class Implementation).
</info added on 2025-08-07T07:16:47.672Z>

subtasks.title: MCP Server Class Implementation
subtasks.description: Create core server class with proper initialization and lifecycle management following MCP specifications
subtasks.details:
Create server.ts with MCPServer class extending SDK base. Implement constructor with ServerOptions configuration. Set up server initialization with proper event handling (onConnect, onDisconnect, onError). Implement graceful shutdown procedures with cleanup handlers. Create server state management for active connections and registered tools. Implement health check endpoint for monitoring. Configure port binding and WebSocket upgrade handling.
<info added on 2025-08-07T08:01:23.814Z>
Implementation completed successfully with AcademicMCPServer class featuring full MCP protocol compliance, comprehensive lifecycle management, robust event handling system, tool registration framework, health monitoring capabilities, graceful shutdown procedures, connection state management, and complete test coverage. Server ready for academic PDF processing integration and Claude Desktop deployment.
</info added on 2025-08-07T08:01:23.814Z>

subtasks.title: Tool Registration Framework
subtasks.description: Design and implement flexible tool registration system for dynamic MCP tool management
subtasks.details: Create ToolRegistry class for managing tool lifecycle. Implement tool interface with name, description, parameters schema, and handler function. Build dynamic tool registration with runtime validation. Create tool discovery mechanism for Claude Desktop. Implement tool versioning and backward compatibility support. Add tool permission system for security. Create tool metadata storage for descriptions and schemas.

subtasks.title: MCP Communication Protocol Setup
subtasks.description: Implement MCP protocol communication layer with proper request/response handling
subtasks.details: Implement JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol handling for MCP communication. Create request router for tool invocations. Implement response serialization with proper MCP formatting. Set up bidirectional streaming for large responses. Handle protocol version negotiation and capability exchange. Implement request validation and sanitization. Create message queue for concurrent request handling.

subtasks.title: Error Handling and Logging Infrastructure
subtasks.description: Build comprehensive error handling and structured logging system for debugging and monitoring
subtasks.details: Implement custom error classes for different failure scenarios. Create error middleware with proper MCP error responses. Set up Winston logger with structured logging format. Implement log levels (debug, info, warn, error) with environment-based configuration. Create request/response logging with correlation IDs. Add performance metrics logging for monitoring. Implement error recovery strategies and circuit breakers.

subtasks.title: Claude Desktop Integration Configuration
subtasks.description: Configure MCP server for seamless Claude Desktop integration with proper manifest and settings
subtasks.details: Create claude_mcp_config.json with server metadata and capabilities. Configure authentication if required by Claude Desktop. Set up development and production environment configurations. Create installation script for Claude Desktop registration. Document configuration options and environment variables. Implement configuration validation on startup. Create troubleshooting guide for common integration issues.

subtasks.title: Testing Infrastructure Setup
subtasks.description: Establish comprehensive testing framework for unit and integration tests with MCP protocol validation
subtasks.details: Set up Jest with TypeScript support for unit testing. Create test utilities for mocking MCP connections and tool calls. Implement integration tests for full server lifecycle. Create protocol compliance tests against MCP specification. Set up test fixtures for various request/response scenarios. Implement load testing for concurrent connections. Configure GitHub Actions for CI/CD with test automation.

tasks.title: Academic PDF Processing Engine
tasks.description: Implement robust PDF text extraction with academic formatting support and structural metadata preservation
tasks.details: Use PyMuPDF and pdfplumber for PDF processing. Extract text while preserving document structure (chapters, sections, paragraphs). Handle multi-column layouts, embedded references, footnotes, and academic publisher formats. Implement fallback strategies for complex layouts. Extract metadata including title, authors, publication info. Create Document class with hierarchical structure representation. Handle encoding issues and special characters common in academic texts.
tasks.testStrategy: Test with diverse academic PDFs from different publishers (IEEE, ACM, Springer, etc.). Verify structure preservation, text quality, and metadata extraction accuracy. Performance tests with large documents.

subtasks.title: PyMuPDF and pdfplumber setup and configuration
subtasks.description: Install, configure and validate PyMuPDF and pdfplumber libraries with academic-specific settings for optimal text extraction
subtasks.details: Install PyMuPDF (fitz) and pdfplumber via pip. Configure extraction settings for academic documents including font recognition, layout analysis parameters, and text flow detection. Set up fallback mechanisms between the two libraries based on document complexity. Create configuration module with publisher-specific extraction profiles.

subtasks.title: Text extraction with layout preservation
subtasks.description: Implement core text extraction logic that maintains document structure and formatting integrity
subtasks.details: Create PDFExtractor class using PyMuPDF as primary extractor with pdfplumber fallback. Implement text extraction methods that preserve paragraph boundaries, maintain reading order, and handle text reflow. Extract text blocks with positional information for later structural analysis. Handle rotated pages and scanned content detection.

subtasks.title: Multi-column layout handling
subtasks.description: Develop algorithms to detect and properly extract text from multi-column academic layouts
subtasks.details: Implement column detection using geometric analysis of text blocks. Create column merging logic that maintains proper reading order. Handle varying column widths and irregular layouts common in academic papers. Support single-column abstracts transitioning to two-column body text. Implement column-spanning elements like figures and tables.

subtasks.title: Academic formatting detection system
subtasks.description: Build detection systems for footnotes, references, equations, and other academic formatting elements
subtasks.details: Implement footnote/endnote detection using font size analysis and positional heuristics. Create equation extraction handling for both inline and display math. Build reference section detection and extraction logic. Identify and extract figure/table captions with proper linking. Handle margin notes and annotations common in humanities texts.

subtasks.title: Metadata extraction system
subtasks.description: Extract and validate document metadata including title, authors, publication information, and DOI
subtasks.details: Extract metadata from PDF info dictionary and XMP metadata. Implement heuristic extraction from first page for title, authors, affiliations. Parse publication information including journal, conference, year, volume, pages. Extract and validate DOI, ISBN, ISSN identifiers. Create fallback extraction using regular expressions and NLP for poorly structured documents.

subtasks.title: Document class with hierarchical structure
subtasks.description: Design and implement Document class that represents the hierarchical structure of academic documents
subtasks.details: Create Document class with nested structure: Document → Chapter → Section → Subsection → Paragraph → Sentence. Implement methods for navigating hierarchy and accessing content at different levels. Store structural metadata including headings, page numbers, and formatting. Support serialization to JSON for persistence. Include methods for structure validation and integrity checking.

subtasks.title: Encoding and special character handling
subtasks.description: Implement robust handling of character encodings and special characters common in academic texts
subtasks.details: Handle UTF-8, Latin-1, and other encodings commonly found in PDFs. Process special characters including mathematical symbols, Greek letters, and diacritical marks. Implement ligature expansion (fi, fl, ff combinations). Handle non-standard fonts and symbol fonts. Create character normalization for consistent downstream processing.

subtasks.title: Publisher-specific format handlers
subtasks.description: Create specialized handlers for IEEE, ACM, Springer, and other major academic publisher formats
subtasks.details: Implement IEEE two-column format handler with specific header/footer processing. Create ACM format handler with proper reference and author block extraction. Build Springer format handler supporting both journal and book chapter layouts. Add Elsevier, Wiley, and Taylor & Francis format support. Create publisher detection logic based on metadata and layout patterns.

subtasks.title: Testing framework with diverse samples
subtasks.description: Build comprehensive testing framework using diverse academic PDF samples from various publishers
subtasks.details: Collect test PDFs from open-access repositories covering all major publishers. Create unit tests for each extraction component (text, structure, metadata). Implement integration tests for end-to-end PDF processing. Build regression tests to prevent quality degradation. Create performance benchmarks for processing speed and memory usage. Validate extraction accuracy against manually annotated ground truth.

tasks.title: Intelligent Text Chunking System
tasks.description: Develop semantic chunking that preserves complete argumentative units and logical flow
tasks.details: Implement semantic chunking using sentence-transformers and spaCy NLP. Preserve complete paragraphs as minimum units, extend to full argumentative sections when needed. Use sliding window approach with overlap to maintain context. Identify natural breakpoints (section headers, topic transitions). Create TextSegment class with parent/child relationships. Implement coherence scoring to validate chunk quality. Handle academic writing patterns (citations, cross-references, formal structure).
tasks.testStrategy: Validate chunk completeness with academic users. Test argument preservation across different text types. Measure coherence scores and optimize thresholds. A/B test against standard chunking methods.

subtasks.title: Set up spaCy NLP pipeline for academic text processing
subtasks.description: Configure and initialize spaCy with appropriate language models and custom components optimized for academic text analysis
subtasks.details: Install spaCy with en_core_web_trf transformer model for better semantic understanding. Create custom pipeline components for academic text patterns including citation detection, formal structure recognition, and theoretical concept identification. Configure tokenization rules to handle academic conventions like abbreviations, author names (et al.), and specialized punctuation. Set up entity recognition for academic entities (authors, institutions, concepts). Initialize linguistic features extraction for dependency parsing and part-of-speech tagging.

subtasks.title: Implement paragraph-level preservation logic
subtasks.description: Develop core logic to maintain complete paragraphs as atomic units during chunking process
subtasks.details: Create ParagraphDetector class that identifies paragraph boundaries using multiple signals: double newlines, indentation patterns, and semantic coherence. Implement minimum chunk size enforcement to never split paragraphs. Develop paragraph metadata extraction including topic sentences, concluding sentences, and internal structure. Handle edge cases like multi-paragraph quotes, indented blocks, and formatted lists. Create validation logic to ensure no partial paragraphs in chunks.

subtasks.title: Build argumentative unit detection using semantic analysis
subtasks.description: Develop algorithms to identify and preserve complete argumentative structures in academic texts
subtasks.details: Implement ArgumentDetector class using discourse markers and rhetorical structure theory. Identify argument components: claims, evidence, warrants, rebuttals, and conclusions. Use dependency parsing to track logical connections between sentences. Detect argument boundaries through transition words, topic shifts, and semantic similarity drops. Create argument graph representation for complex multi-paragraph arguments. Handle nested arguments and sub-arguments common in academic writing.

subtasks.title: Implement sliding window with configurable overlap
subtasks.description: Create flexible sliding window mechanism for chunk generation with context preservation
subtasks.details: Develop SlidingWindowChunker with configurable window size (500-2000 tokens) and overlap percentage (10-30%). Implement dynamic window adjustment based on content density and argument complexity. Create overlap deduplication strategy to avoid redundant content in vector store. Build context preservation logic ensuring overlaps contain complete sentences. Implement window boundary optimization to align with natural breakpoints. Add configuration for different academic disciplines with varying optimal parameters.

subtasks.title: Develop natural breakpoint identification algorithms
subtasks.description: Create sophisticated algorithms to identify optimal chunk boundaries at natural discourse transitions
subtasks.details: Implement BreakpointDetector using multiple signals: section headers, topic modeling transitions, semantic similarity valleys, and discourse markers. Create hierarchy-aware breaking that respects document structure levels. Develop topic coherence scoring to identify thematic boundaries. Use sentence embeddings to detect semantic shifts between paragraphs. Implement LaTeX/Markdown structure recognition for formatted documents. Handle special academic structures like theorems, proofs, and definitions.

subtasks.title: Create TextSegment class with relationship management
subtasks.description: Design and implement comprehensive TextSegment data structure with hierarchical and referential relationships
subtasks.details: Develop TextSegment class with attributes: content, start/end positions, document_id, parent_id, child_ids, sibling_ids, and semantic_neighbors. Implement relationship tracking for parent/child hierarchies and cross-references. Create segment metadata including type (paragraph, section, argument), confidence scores, and embedding vectors. Build segment tree structure for efficient navigation. Implement serialization for storage and retrieval. Add methods for context expansion and relationship traversal.

subtasks.title: Build coherence scoring system
subtasks.description: Develop metrics and scoring system to validate chunk quality and semantic coherence
subtasks.details: Implement CoherenceScorer using multiple metrics: semantic similarity between sentences, topic consistency scores, argument completeness indicators, and citation integrity checks. Create coherence threshold validation with configurable minimum scores. Develop chunk quality metrics including readability, self-containment, and context sufficiency. Build feedback loop for chunk boundary adjustment based on low scores. Implement comparative scoring against baseline chunking methods. Add logging and monitoring for quality assurance.

subtasks.title: Implement academic citation and cross-reference pattern recognition
subtasks.description: Create specialized handlers for academic writing patterns including citations and cross-references
subtasks.details: Develop CitationPatternRecognizer for inline citations (Author, Year), footnotes, and numbered references. Implement CrossReferenceDetector for internal document references ('see Section 2.3', 'as discussed above'). Create citation context preservation ensuring citations remain with their referencing text. Build cross-reference mapping table for later resolution. Handle various citation styles (APA, MLA, Chicago, IEEE). Implement confidence scoring for ambiguous references. Ensure citations and references are never orphaned in chunks.

tasks.title: Academic-Optimized Embedding Generation
tasks.description: Generate high-quality embeddings optimized for academic language and theoretical concepts
tasks.details: Use sentence-transformers with academic-trained models (all-MiniLM-L6-v2 fine-tuned on scholarly texts, or specialized models like SciBERT). Implement domain-specific embedding optimization for humanities and social sciences. Create embedding pipeline that handles theoretical terminology, author names, and concept relationships. Store embeddings with FAISS for efficient similarity search. Implement embedding quality validation and confidence scoring.
tasks.testStrategy: Test semantic similarity on academic concepts. Compare retrieval quality against generic embeddings. Validate with discipline-specific terminology. Performance benchmarks for embedding generation speed.

subtasks.title: Set up sentence-transformers environment and select academic models
subtasks.description: Install sentence-transformers library, evaluate and select appropriate models for academic text (SciBERT, all-MiniLM-L6-v2, or fine-tuned variants)
subtasks.details: Research and compare academic-specific embedding models including SciBERT, SciNCL, and SPECTER. Set up Python environment with sentence-transformers, evaluate model performance on sample academic texts. Document model selection criteria and rationale for chosen models.

subtasks.title: Implement domain-specific optimization for humanities/social sciences
subtasks.description: Create optimization layer for theoretical terminology and discipline-specific language patterns
subtasks.details: Build terminology preprocessor for handling theoretical concepts, author names, and citation patterns. Implement specialized tokenization for humanities texts (e.g., German/French terms, philosophical concepts). Create mapping for discipline-specific abbreviations and conventions.

subtasks.title: Build embedding pipeline with batch processing
subtasks.description: Develop efficient pipeline for generating embeddings from text segments with batch processing capabilities
subtasks.details: Create EmbeddingPipeline class with batch processing support for handling multiple text segments efficiently. Implement async processing for large documents. Add progress tracking and memory management for processing entire academic libraries. Include error handling and retry logic.

subtasks.title: Integrate FAISS vector store and implement indexing
subtasks.description: Set up FAISS for efficient vector storage and similarity search with proper indexing strategies
subtasks.details: Install and configure FAISS library, create VectorStore class wrapping FAISS functionality. Implement IVF indexing for large-scale search. Add metadata storage alongside vectors for document references. Create index persistence and loading mechanisms.

subtasks.title: Develop confidence scoring mechanism for embedding quality
subtasks.description: Create system to evaluate and score embedding quality for academic content reliability
subtasks.details: Implement confidence scoring based on embedding coherence, semantic density, and domain-specific metrics. Create validation against known academic concept relationships. Add quality thresholds for filtering low-confidence embeddings. Include scoring visualization for debugging.

subtasks.title: Optimize performance for large-scale embedding generation
subtasks.description: Implement performance optimizations for processing entire academic libraries efficiently
subtasks.details: Add GPU acceleration support for embedding generation. Implement caching strategies for frequently accessed embeddings. Optimize batch sizes and memory usage. Create incremental indexing for new documents. Add performance monitoring and benchmarking tools.

tasks.title: Context-Aware Search Implementation
tasks.description: Build semantic search system that finds relevant content with full surrounding context
tasks.details: Implement hybrid search combining semantic similarity (FAISS) with keyword matching. Create SearchEngine class with query preprocessing, embedding-based retrieval, and result ranking. Preserve full context around matches (paragraph-level minimum). Implement relevance scoring with confidence indicators. Support natural language queries and academic terminology. Add query expansion for related terms and synonyms. Create result caching for performance.
tasks.testStrategy: Test with diverse academic queries. Measure precision/recall against manual relevance judgments. Validate context preservation and relevance scoring. Performance tests with target <3s response time.

subtasks.title: Design Hybrid Search Architecture
subtasks.description: Design and implement the hybrid search architecture combining semantic and keyword matching strategies
subtasks.details: Create SearchEngine base class with abstract methods for semantic and keyword search. Design HybridSearchStrategy class that combines FAISS vector similarity with BM25/TF-IDF keyword matching. Implement weight configuration for balancing semantic vs keyword scores. Design interfaces for query processing, result merging, and score normalization. Create architecture for pluggable search strategies.

subtasks.title: Implement Query Preprocessing and Expansion
subtasks.description: Build query preprocessing pipeline with academic term expansion and synonym handling
subtasks.details: Create QueryProcessor class with tokenization, stemming, and stopword removal for academic texts. Implement academic term expansion using domain-specific thesaurus and ontologies. Build synonym detection using WordNet and academic terminology databases. Create query rewriting module for handling complex academic phrases. Implement query normalization for handling different citation formats and author names.

subtasks.title: Integrate FAISS Vector Search
subtasks.description: Set up FAISS integration for efficient vector similarity search on academic embeddings
subtasks.details: Install and configure FAISS for Python. Create FAISSIndex wrapper class for managing vector indices. Implement index building from document embeddings with proper dimensionality. Configure index type (IVF, HNSW) based on corpus size and performance requirements. Implement k-NN search with configurable k parameter. Add index persistence and loading mechanisms.

subtasks.title: Build Result Ranking Algorithm
subtasks.description: Develop sophisticated ranking algorithm with relevance scoring for academic search results
subtasks.details: Implement multi-factor ranking algorithm combining semantic similarity, keyword relevance, document importance, and recency. Create RelevanceScorer class with configurable weights for different factors. Implement learning-to-rank features for academic domain. Add boost factors for exact phrase matches, title matches, and author relevance. Create score normalization and result re-ranking capabilities.

subtasks.title: Implement Context Preservation System
subtasks.description: Build system to preserve and return full paragraph-level context around search matches
subtasks.details: Create ContextExtractor class that retrieves surrounding paragraphs for each match. Implement smart boundary detection to avoid breaking logical units mid-thought. Add configurable context window sizes (sentences before/after, full paragraph, section). Build context highlighting system to mark exact matches within context. Implement context deduplication for overlapping results.

subtasks.title: Add Confidence Indicators
subtasks.description: Implement confidence scoring system for search results and relevance indicators
subtasks.details: Create ConfidenceCalculator class computing scores based on multiple factors (semantic similarity, keyword overlap, structural match). Implement confidence bands (high/medium/low) with configurable thresholds. Add explainability features showing why results have certain confidence levels. Create confidence aggregation for multi-source results. Implement confidence calibration using validation datasets.

subtasks.title: Implement Caching Layer
subtasks.description: Build performance optimization caching system for frequently accessed queries and results
subtasks.details: Implement Redis-based caching for query results with TTL configuration. Create QueryCache class with LRU eviction policy. Build cache key generation considering query normalization and parameters. Implement cache invalidation on document updates. Add cache warming for common academic queries. Create cache hit/miss metrics and monitoring.

subtasks.title: Create Natural Language Query Parser
subtasks.description: Build NLP-based query parser optimized for academic search queries
subtasks.details: Implement NLQueryParser using spaCy/NLTK for parsing natural language academic queries. Extract entities (authors, concepts, dates) and intent (definition, comparison, citation). Handle complex academic query patterns ('What does X say about Y?', 'Compare A and B on topic C'). Create query templates for common academic search patterns. Implement query suggestion and auto-completion features.

tasks.title: Source Attribution and Citation System
tasks.description: Implement exact source attribution with page/paragraph references and citation formatting
tasks.details: Create citation extraction using pybtex and citeproc-py. Map text segments to exact page and paragraph locations. Extract bibliographic metadata from PDFs and validate against standard formats. Implement Citation class with format validation (APA, MLA, Chicago, etc.). Create source attribution tracking for every text segment. Support export to BibTeX, Zotero, Mendeley formats. Ensure 100% accuracy in page/paragraph references.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify citation accuracy with manual validation. Test format compatibility with reference managers. Validate page/paragraph references across different PDF structures. Zero-tolerance testing for citation errors.

tasks.title: MCP Tool Integration Layer
tasks.description: Integrate search and citation functionality into MCP tools for Claude Desktop interaction
tasks.details: Create MCP tools for literature_search, document_retrieval, citation_lookup, and source_attribution. Implement proper error handling and validation for tool calls. Create structured response formats that Claude can effectively use. Implement streaming responses for large results. Add tool descriptions and parameter schemas. Create integration tests with actual Claude Desktop sessions. Handle concurrent requests and resource management.
tasks.testStrategy: Integration testing with Claude Desktop. Validate tool response formats and error handling. Test concurrent usage scenarios. User acceptance testing with natural language queries through Claude.

tasks.title: Document Structure Intelligence
tasks.description: Implement hierarchical document understanding and cross-reference resolution
tasks.details: Create DocumentStructure class for hierarchical navigation (chapter → section → subsection). Implement cross-reference resolution for 'see Chapter 2', 'as discussed above' patterns using NLP and structural analysis. Extract and link footnotes/endnotes to main text. Create terminology mapping system to track author-specific language usage. Build confidence scoring for cross-reference resolution. Handle different academic document structures and conventions.
tasks.testStrategy: Test cross-reference resolution accuracy with diverse academic texts. Validate structural extraction across different document types. Measure confidence scores and optimize resolution algorithms.

tasks.title: Multi-Source Synthesis Engine
tasks.description: Build capabilities for argument tracking, contradiction detection, and research gap analysis
tasks.details: Implement ArgumentTracker class for chronological concept development across authors. Create ContradictionDetector using semantic similarity and sentiment analysis to identify opposing viewpoints. Build ResearchGapAnalyzer to identify underexplored areas by analyzing concept coverage and methodology patterns. Create synthesis validation using coherence metrics. Support multi-document narrative construction with proper attribution. Implement confidence scoring for synthesis quality.
tasks.testStrategy: Validate argument tracking with known theoretical developments. Test contradiction detection against manually identified disagreements. Evaluate research gap suggestions with domain experts. Measure synthesis coherence and quality.

tasks.title: Performance Optimization and Production Readiness
tasks.description: Optimize system performance for production use with 50+ PDFs and implement comprehensive error handling
tasks.details: Implement caching strategies for embeddings, search results, and processed documents. Optimize memory usage with lazy loading and efficient data structures. Add batch processing capabilities for systematic reviews. Implement comprehensive error handling, logging, and recovery mechanisms. Create monitoring and metrics collection. Optimize for target performance: <3s search response, <2GB memory usage, support for 500MB-1GB document libraries. Add configuration management and deployment scripts.
tasks.testStrategy: Load testing with 50+ PDFs. Memory usage profiling and optimization. Performance benchmarking against targets. Stress testing with concurrent users. Error injection testing for robustness validation.
